message:
feat(frankenpandas): expose Series-level Arrow interop (fd90.264 / br-5x2tp)

The README at line 1580 documents Arrow interop ("DataFrame ↔ Arrow
RecordBatch conversion is built in"). The DataFrame-level path is
exposed via the existing read_feather/read_parquet IO surface, but the
Series-level Arrow interop functions were unreachable from
frankenpandas:

- series_to_arrow_array — Series → (ArrowDataType, Arc<dyn Array>)
- series_from_arrow_array — Arrow array → Series

Useful for users plugging into Arrow-compatible systems (DuckDB,
DataFusion, Spark via Arrow Flight) at the Series granularity.

Kept out of prelude — specialized API; most users will use the
Parquet/Feather IO instead.

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-5tz7","title":"pandas/io/excel: read_excel(header=None, names=[...]) explicit column naming parity","description":"py signature:
pandas.read_excel(io, header=None, names=["a", "b"], index_col=None, skiprows=0) -> DataFrame

Current fp-io has only has_headers: bool and autogenerated column_N names. Add pandas parity for header=None plus explicit names override and validate width mismatches.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:23:31.640873934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T11:40:42.644459282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T11:40:42.642163578Z","close_reason":"Implemented explicit names for headerless Excel reads in commit d071612","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-5vg7r","title":"fd90.111: README case_when example uses non-existent Series::constant","description":"fd90.74-110 README audit follow-on. Lines 1070-1071 in case_when example also reference Series::constant(name, len), but no such function exists in the public API:

  grep -rn 'fn constant' crates/ → only a private helper in proptest_properties.rs:1103, NOT a Series method.

A user copy-pasting would hit:
  error[E0599]: no function or associated item named 'constant' found
                for struct 'Series'

The cleanest example for case_when uses Series::from_values with a Vec<Scalar> of identical values (the explicit construction pattern users would write themselves). Alternative: refer the reader to construct the value Series themselves and just show case_when's structure.

Fix: rewrite the case_when example to build the value Series via Series::from_values with an inline vec![Scalar::Utf8(...); n] pattern. Show the actual construction so the example is genuinely runnable. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:41:11.477170377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:42:28.726185798Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:42:28.723512787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-5x2tp","title":"fd90.264: Expose series_from_arrow_array + series_to_arrow_array (Arrow interop)","description":"fd90.264: Expose series_from_arrow_array / series_to_arrow_array (Arrow interop).

The README at line 1580 documents Arrow interop ("DataFrame ↔ Arrow RecordBatch conversion is built in"). Two fp-io functions for Series-level Arrow interop are unreachable from frankenpandas:

- series_to_arrow_array (line 2147) — Series → (ArrowDataType, Arc<dyn Array>)
- series_from_arrow_array (line 2153) — Arrow array → Series

These complete the Arrow interop surface for users wanting to plug into Arrow-compatible systems (DuckDB, DataFusion, Spark via Arrow Flight per README).

Scope:
- Add 2 functions to top-level fp_io re-export
- Skip prelude (specialized — most users will use Parquet/Feather IO, not raw Arrow)

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.

 {"id":"frankenpandas-5zni","title":"fd90.90: README test count badge says 1500+ but body says 3,200+ (badge grossly stale)","description":"fd90.74-89 README audit continuation. Top-of-README badge (line 12) claims 'tests-1500+' but body text at line 867 ('3,200+ tests across the workspace') and FAQ line 1448 ('3,200+ tests') give a much higher figure. A spot-check sums the per-crate breakdown table (lines 871-884) to ~3,392 distinct test items, and a permissive grep across #[test]/#[tokio::test]/#[rstest] returns 3,711. The badge is grossly stale relative to body text it shares the same page with. Fix: bump badge from 1500+ to 3200+ to match the lower-bound figure already cited twice in body text. Pure README change, no code impact.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:02:33.001930012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:03:20.179918621Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:03:20.177282309Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
